cot_prefix: Okay, let's look at the question and the text carefully. The question is about why the author said a loud "thank you" to the doorman. The options are A to D. Let me go through the story again to understand the context. The author, on a road trip, stopped for lunch. There was a man with a dirty face who opened the door for them. The kids noticed he smelled and were rude. The author tried to set a good example. Later, when she went back to the car for car-sick pills, the doorman was opening the door again, and she said a loud thank you. Then she bought him food after explaining his situation to her family. The kids then understood and smiled when they heard the order was for him. The author ended up hugging him, realizing the importance of gratitude and setting an example for her children. The key part here is the author's motivation for saying "thank you" loudly. At the time, she went out to get the pills, and the doorman opened the door again. She said thank you, which was in front of her kids. The story later shows that she wanted to teach her children about gratitude by example, as she later buys him food and explains his situation. The purpose of the loud thank you was likely to demonstrate gratitude in front of her children as a lesson. Looking at the options: A. To give the children a lesson of gratitude. This fits because the story emphasizes teaching by example. B. Show real love. The story doesn't mention love, more about gratitude and setting an example. C. Give the doorman a hand. She did that later by ordering food, but the thank you was earlier. D. Polite manner. While politeness is part of it, the story focuses more on teaching the kids. So, the answer is A. The author wanted to teach her kids about gratitude by example. The loud thank you was a deliberate act to show them how to be
